steps.title: Spot the clause boundary
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: Chambers did not play drums on "Rapper's Delight," and he joined Sugar Hill Records after the song's release. The transition however is set off after the first clause, and a semicolon is needed before the second independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation
steps.content: Delight,” however; correctly places however after the first clause and uses the semicolon to join the two independent clauses. The answer is C.
